Defines memory regions for firmware scratch and safe firmware storage.

# Purpose
This C header file defines memory regions for firmware storage in a system. It specifies two main regions: `FLASH_REGION_FIRMWARE_SCRATCH` and `FLASH_REGION_SAFE_FIRMWARE`. The `FLASH_REGION_FIRMWARE_SCRATCH` is intended for temporary storage of firmware images, both normal and recovery, and spans from address `0x0` to `0x80000`, which is 512 kilobytes. The `FLASH_REGION_SAFE_FIRMWARE` is designated for stable firmware storage, starting at address `0x200000` and ending at `0x280000`, also covering 512 kilobytes. These definitions help manage memory allocation for firmware updates and recovery processes.
